title: Dryopteris mannii
taxonomy: Dryopteris mannii is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Dryopteris, which includes over 250 species of ferns. The family Dryopteridaceae is a group of ferns that are commonly known as wood ferns or buckler ferns. Dryopteris mannii is native to Japan and is classified as a vascular plant, a type of plant that has a vascular system for transporting water, nutrients, and sugars. As a member of the kingdom Plantae, Dryopteris mannii is also classified as a flowering plant, although it produces spores rather than flowers.
uses: Dryopteris mannii, also known as Mann's wood fern, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. Its leaves have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. In some cultures, it is also used to aid in the treatment of skin conditions and wounds. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal properties and potential uses. As with any plant, it's essential to consult with a medical professional before using it for medicinal purposes.

parts: Dryopteris mannii, a species of fern, has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in China, where its rhizome is considered a valuable medicinal part. preparations: Dryopteris mannii is a plant species used in traditional herbal medicine, particularly in Asia.
